meta.name: Spatially variable genes (sepal)
meta.description: Score genes by how far their expression pattern is from a diffused (spatially random) steady state — the sepal score. Higher = more spatially structured. Ranking lands in uns['sepal_score'].
meta.provenance: squidpy 'Compute Sepal score' example (squidpy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/notebooks/examples/graph/compute_sepal.html). Targets the visium_hne example.

readme: Builds a hexagonal-grid spatial graph, then scores spatial structure with the sepal diffusion method. `max_neighs=6` matches the Visium hexagonal grid (sepal only accepts grid-like graphs). Runs single-process (`n_jobs=1`) per the squidpy example; scores every gene, so it is slower than Moran's I / Geary's C.
